producer.name: Penfolds
producer.philosophy: Australia's most iconic and collected winery, founded by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold in 1844 at Magill Estate, Adelaide. The house style is built on multi-region blending — sourcing from Barossa Valley, McLaren Vale, Clare Valley, Coonawarra, and Adelaide Hills to achieve consistency and complexity across vintages. Penfolds pioneered the use of American oak hogsheads for Shiraz (Grange) and has maintained a bin numbering system since the 1960s to classify wines by style and quality tier. The Rewards of Patience tastings, held every five years, re-evaluate back vintages — a unique commitment to long-term quality tracking. Max Schubert's experimental Grange 1951 was hidden from management for years before becoming Australia's greatest wine.

metadata.founder: Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold & Mary Penfold
metadata.winemaking: Multi-district blending across South Australia is the defining philosophy. American oak hogsheads (300L) are central to the Grange and Bin programs. French oak barriques used for Yattarna and RWT. Extended maceration for reds. Open and headed-down fermentation. The bin numbering system tracks wine lineage and style across decades.
metadata.notable_recognition: Grange is the only wine outside France to be listed as a Heritage Icon by the South Australian National Trust. Max Schubert awarded CBE for services to the wine industry. Multiple wines classified Exceptional by Langton's Classification of Australian Wine.
metadata.historical_note: Dr. Penfold originally planted vines to produce fortified wines for medicinal purposes. His wife Mary ran the winery after his death in 1870 and expanded production. Max Schubert created Grange in 1951 after visiting Bordeaux, but management ordered him to stop. He secretly continued making Grange, and the 1955 vintage won a gold medal that vindicated the project. Grange has been produced continuously since 1952 (except 1957 and 1964, when no wine met the standard).

metadata.title: Chief Winemaker
metadata.notes: Joined Penfolds in 1989 as a red winemaker. Appointed Chief Winemaker in 2002, succeeding John Duval. Trained as a secondary school teacher before pivoting to oenology at Roseworthy Agricultural College. Responsible for final blending decisions on Grange and all Penfolds wines. Known for meticulous blending trials and stewardship of the Rewards of Patience program.

wines.name: Grange
wines.vinification_notes: Multi-district blend sourcing from Barossa Valley, McLaren Vale, Clare Valley, and Magill Estate. Fermented in stainless steel and headed-down open fermenters. 18 months maturation in 100% new American oak hogsheads (300L). The American oak regime is integral — Grange is defined by its oak character as much as its fruit. Component wines are blended after oak maturation. Small percentages of Cabernet Sauvignon are included in most vintages. Previously labeled 'Grange Hermitage' until 1990.
wines.soil_description: Varies by source district — Barossa Valley red-brown earth over limestone; McLaren Vale clay loam over limestone and ironstone; Magill Estate alluvial loam over quartzite

wines.name: Bin 707 Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.vinification_notes: The Cabernet counterpart to Grange. Multi-district blend sourced primarily from Coonawarra, Barossa Valley, and Padthaway. Fermented in stainless steel. 18 months maturation in new American oak hogsheads (300L). Not produced in vintages where fruit quality is insufficient — skipped 1971-1975, 1981-1982, 1995-1996, 2003, and 2011.
wines.soil_description: Coonawarra terra rossa over limestone; Barossa Valley red-brown earth; Padthaway loam over limestone

wines.name: RWT Bin 798 Barossa Valley Shiraz
wines.vinification_notes: RWT stands for 'Red Winemaking Trial' — originally an experimental counterpoint to Grange using French oak instead of American. Sourced exclusively from Barossa Valley vineyards. Fermented in stainless steel and open fermenters. 15-17 months maturation in French oak hogsheads (300L), approximately 70% new. The wine that proved Barossa Shiraz works brilliantly with French oak — more restrained, structural, and savory than the American oak-driven Grange.
wines.soil_description: Barossa Valley red-brown earth and sandy loam over clay, some terra rossa sections

wines.name: St Henri Shiraz
wines.vinification_notes: The philosophical opposite of Grange — multi-district Shiraz matured for 12 months in old, large 1,460-litre oak vats with no new oak at all. This allows pure fruit and terroir expression without oak influence. Originally created by John Davoren in the 1950s. The wine is deliberately reductive in youth, evolving complex secondary and tertiary characters with extended cellaring. Small percentages of Cabernet Sauvignon may be included.

wines.name: Bin 389 Cabernet Shiraz
wines.vinification_notes: Known as 'Baby Grange' — matured in the same American oak hogsheads used for the previous vintage of Grange (one-year-old seasoned oak). Multi-district blend from across South Australia. Fermented in stainless steel. 12 months in American oak hogsheads. The bridge between the Bin range and the luxury wines — accessible young but rewards cellaring. Created by Max Schubert in 1960.

wines.name: Bin 28 Kalimna Shiraz
wines.vinification_notes: Named after the Kalimna vineyard in the Barossa Valley, though now a multi-district blend. Matured for 12 months in seasoned American oak hogsheads. Warm, generous, and approachable Shiraz — the workhorse red of the Penfolds range. Fermented in stainless steel with pump-overs. The original Kalimna vineyard, purchased in 1945, supplied fruit for early Grange vintages.
wines.soil_description: Barossa Valley red-brown earth and sandy loam

wines.name: Bin 128 Coonawarra Shiraz
wines.vinification_notes: Single-region Shiraz from Coonawarra in South Australia's Limestone Coast. Matured for 12 months in French oak hogsheads, approximately 25% new. A cooler-climate, more elegant style of Shiraz compared to the Barossa-driven Bin 28. Fermented in stainless steel. French oak (rather than American) reflects the more restrained Coonawarra fruit profile.
wines.soil_description: Coonawarra terra rossa — thin layer of red loam over ancient limestone, excellent drainage and heat retention

wines.name: Bin 311 Chardonnay
wines.vinification_notes: Multi-district blend sourced from Tumbarumba (NSW), Adelaide Hills, and Henty (Victoria). Whole-bunch pressed. Fermented in French oak barriques, approximately 30% new. 8 months on lees with regular stirring. Partial malolactic fermentation. A relatively recent addition to the Penfolds range, designed as a cool-climate, restrained Chardonnay — the white counterpart to the Bin reds.

wines.name: Yattarna Chardonnay
wines.vinification_notes: Penfolds' flagship white — the Chardonnay equivalent of Grange in ambition. Name means 'gradually' in the Peramangk Aboriginal language, reflecting years of trial vinifications before the first release. Multi-district blend from Tasmania, Adelaide Hills, and Tumbarumba. Whole-bunch pressed. Fermented in French oak barriques, approximately 40% new. 8-9 months on lees with regular stirring. Full malolactic fermentation. The culmination of Penfolds' 'white wine trial' program initiated in the 1990s.

wines.name: Bin 51 Riesling
wines.vinification_notes: Single-region Riesling from Eden Valley in the Barossa zone. Cold-fermented in stainless steel to preserve varietal purity. No oak, no malolactic. Bottled early to retain freshness and acidity. Classic Eden Valley style — lime, floral, and mineral with a dry, crisp finish. The only aromatic white in the Penfolds range.
wines.soil_description: Eden Valley sandy loam and quartz gravel over ancient schist and gneiss

wines.name: Koonunga Hill Shiraz Cabernet
wines.vinification_notes: The entry point to Penfolds — named after a vineyard near the original Magill Estate. Multi-district blend from across South Australia. Fermented in stainless steel. 10-12 months maturation in seasoned American and French oak. Designed to be approachable and fruit-forward in youth. Introduced in 1976 to provide an affordable everyday red that still carries the Penfolds hallmark of quality and consistency.
